title: Daily GK Quiz: 10 Questions for Monday, 20 July 2026
meta_description: Test your general knowledge with 10 questions on History, Geography, Polity, Science, Sports, and Current Affairs for 20 July 2026.
html_body:
Hey there, smart friend! Let's test your Gyaan with today's quiz. We've got a mix of History, Geography, Polity, Science, Sports, and a bit of current affairs. Ready? Let's go!

Q1. Which ancient Indian university, known as a center of learning, was invaded and destroyed by Bakhtiyar Khilji in 1200 CE?

Answer: Nalanda University

Nalanda was a famous Mahavihara (Buddhist monastery-university) in ancient Magadha, now in Bihar. It attracted students from all over Asia. The destruction by Khilji's forces marked a big setback for Buddhist learning in India. The university's ruins are now a UNESCO World Heritage Site. This event also contributed to the decline of Buddhism in the Indian subcontinent.

Q2. What is the capital of the Indian state of Mizoram?

Answer: Aizawl

Aizawl is the capital city of Mizoram, located in the northeastern part of India. It's a beautiful hill city surrounded by lush green mountains. Mizoram became a state in 1987, and Aizawl is its cultural and administrative center. The city is known for its vibrant festivals like Chapchar Kut and for being home to the Mizoram State Museum.

Q3. Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the abolition of untouchability?

Answer: Article 17

Article 17 abolishes 'untouchability' and forbids its practice in any form. It's a fundamental right under the Right to Equality. The enforcement of this article is a key step towards social justice in India. Violations of this article can be challenged in courts. This article reflects the Constitution's commitment to equality for all citizens.

Q4. What is the chemical symbol for gold?

Answer: Au

Gold's symbol 'Au' comes from its Latin name 'Aurum'. It's a precious metal that's been valuable to humans for thousands of years. Gold is highly malleable and doesn't tarnish, making it perfect for jewelry and coins. In India, gold is especially important in festivals and weddings. The atomic number of gold is 79.

Q5. Which Indian cricketer holds the record for the most runs in Test cricket?

Answer: Sachin Tendulkar

Sachin Tendulkar scored 15,921 runs in Test cricket during his incredible career. He played 200 Test matches for India, a record in itself. Tendulkar is often called the 'God of Cricket' in India for his unmatched achievements. He retired in 2013 after a glorious 24-year international career. His consistency over decades is what makes his record truly special.

Q6. The River Nile flows through how many African countries?

Answer: 11 countries

The Nile flows through 11 countries including Egypt, Sudan, South Sudan, Ethiopia, Uganda, and others. It's the longest river in Africa and crucial for agriculture and drinking water. The White Nile and Blue Nile are its main tributaries. The river has been vital for civilizations since ancient times, especially in Egypt. Its source is in Burundi, and it flows into the Mediterranean Sea.

Q7. Who was the first woman Governor of an Indian state?

Actually in our structure we use

for all questions, so correcting:

Q7. Who was the first woman Governor of an Indian state?

Answer: Sarojini Naidu

Sarojini Naidu became the Governor of Uttar Pradesh in 1947, making her the first woman to hold this office in India. She was a freedom fighter, poet, and orator, often called the 'Nightingale of India'. Her appointment as Governor was a significant step for women's representation in high offices. She served until 1949. Naidu was also a key figure in the Quit India Movement.

Q8. What is the main component of natural gas used for cooking?

Answer: Methane

Methane (CH4) is the primary component of natural gas, usually making up about 70-90% of it. It's a clean-burning fossil fuel that's piped into homes for cooking and heating. In India, the government promotes the use of PNG (Piped Natural Gas) in cities for cleaner energy. Methane is also a potent greenhouse gas, so controlling leaks is important for the environment.

Q9. Which mountain range separates Europe from Asia?

Answer: The Ural Mountains

The Ural Mountains run through western Russia and are traditionally considered the boundary between Europe and Asia. They stretch about 2,500 km from the Arctic Ocean to the Ural River. The range is rich in minerals like iron, gold, and copper. It's one of the world's oldest mountain ranges, formed around 250 to 300 million years ago. The highest peak is Mount Narodnaya.

Q10. What is the name of India's first indigenous aircraft carrier, commissioned in 2022?

Answer: INS Vikrant

INS Vikrant is India's first indigenous aircraft carrier, built by Cochin Shipyard Limited. It was commissioned in September 2022 and is a major milestone for India's naval capabilities. The carrier can operate MiG-29K fighter jets and other aircraft. Vikrant enhances India's ability to project power in the Indian Ocean Region. Its name honors India's first aircraft carrier, which played a key role in the 1971 war.

faq.q: What topics are covered in this quiz?
faq.a: This quiz covers History, Geography, Polity, Science, Sports, and Current Affairs related to India.

faq.q: How can I use this quiz to improve my GK?
faq.a: Try to answer each question before reading the explanation. Then read the context to understand why the answer matters. This helps in retaining information better.

faq.q: Are these questions relevant for competitive exams?
faq.a: Yes, these questions cover fundamental topics often asked in exams like UPSC, SSC, and state PSCs. The explanations provide additional context that's useful for mains answers.

faq.q: How often should I take such quizzes?
faq.a: Regular practice is key. Try to do one quiz daily or every alternate day to build and maintain your general knowledge.

fact_sources.claim: Nalanda University was destroyed by Bakhtiyar Khilji in 1200 CE
Aizawl is the capital of Mizoram
Article 17 of Indian Constitution abolishes untouchability
Chemical symbol for gold is Au
Sachin Tendulkar has the most runs in Test cricket
River Nile flows through 11 African countries
Sarojini Naidu was the first woman Governor of an Indian state
Methane is the main component of natural gas
Ural Mountains separate Europe from Asia
INS Vikrant is India's first indigenous aircraft carrier
